intro: Pseudobarleria rupestris, commonly known as the rock barleria, is a plant species belonging to the Acanthaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can be utilized to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseudobarleria rupestris include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical ointments and creams, while the dried leaves can be used to make tea or added to herbal remedies.
